syntax.maximum_complexity: ordinary spoken instructions at or below the approved FASTQ sentence level
syntax.publication_rule: A sentence is listed only when the browser and computer runtimes both support it.

commands.label: Require a condition
commands.example: Make sure at least 4 reads remain.

commands.label: Show a warning
commands.example: Show a warning saying This sample needs review.

commands.label: Choose between two paths
commands.example:
If the result is not empty:
    Say A result was found.
Otherwise:
    Say No result was found.

commands.label: Add another decision choice
commands.example:
If fewer than 10 reads remain:
    Say Very few reads remain.
Otherwise if fewer than 100 reads remain:
    Say A small number of reads remain.
Otherwise:
    Say Many reads remain.

commands.label: Open a sample collection
commands.example: Open all FASTQ files as samples.

commands.label: Run instructions for every sample
commands.example:
For every sample in samples:
    Open the sample.
    Count the reads.

commands.label: Make and use a recipe
commands.example:
Make a recipe called Prepare sample:
    Check the file.
    Count the file.

Use the recipe Prepare sample.

commands.label: Open paired FASTQ files
commands.example: Open the files forward.fastq and reverse.fastq as a pair.

commands.label: Open files together
commands.example: Open the files first.fasta and second.fasta together.

commands.label: Merge files
commands.example: Merge the files first.fasta and second.fasta.

commands.label: Merge another file into the result
Keep matching rows
commands.example: Keep only rows marked treated under condition.

commands.label: Remove matching rows
commands.example: Remove rows marked failed under status.

commands.label: Keep selected columns
commands.example: Keep only the columns sample, condition, and status.

commands.label: Rename a column
commands.example: Rename the column condition to group.

commands.label: Order rows by a column
commands.example: Put the rows in order by age.

commands.label: Replace empty values
commands.example: Replace empty values under status with unknown.

commands.label: Combine matching tables
commands.example: Combine it with metadata.csv using sample.

commands.label: Change a table value
commands.example: Change control to untreated under condition.

commands.label: Keep long sequences
commands.example: Keep only sequences longer than 500 bases.

commands.label: Keep a minimum sequence length
commands.example: Keep sequences at least 100 bases long.

commands.label: Rename a sequence
commands.example: Rename the sequence sample-17 to treated-17.

commands.label: Add text before sequence names
commands.example: Add treated- to the start of every sequence name.

commands.label: Add text after sequence names
commands.example: Add -clean to the end of every sequence name.

commands.label: Keep matching sequence names
commands.example: Keep sequences with names containing treated.

commands.label: Remove matching sequence names
commands.example: Remove sequences with names containing failed.

commands.label: Limit ambiguous bases
commands.example: Keep sequences with at most 2 ambiguous bases.

commands.label: Split sequences into files
commands.example: Split the sequences into files with 100 sequences each as split.fasta.

commands.label: Keep high-quality reads
commands.example: Keep reads with average quality at least 20.

commands.label: Remove reads below a quality
commands.example: Remove reads with average quality below 20.

commands.label: Cut bases from read starts
commands.example: Cut 10 bases from the beginning of each read.

commands.label: Cut bases from read ends
commands.example: Cut 5 bases from the end of each read.

commands.label: Assemble a bacterial genome from paired reads
commands.example: Assemble the bacterial genome from forward.fastq and reverse.fastq into assembly.

commands.label: Assemble a bacterial genome from one file
commands.example: Assemble the bacterial genome from reads.fastq into assembly.

commands.label: Check a bacterial assembly
commands.example: Check the assembly assembly/contigs.fasta into assembly-quality.

commands.label: Annotate a bacterial genome
commands.example: Annotate the bacterial genome assembly/contigs.fasta into annotation.

commands.label: Find resistance genes
commands.example: Find resistance genes in assembly/contigs.fasta using resistance-markers.

commands.label: Identify an organism
commands.example: Identify the organism in reads.fastq using bacteria-reference.

commands.label: Find plasmids
commands.example: Find plasmids in assembly/contigs.fasta into plasmids.
